files.content:
{
  "name": "pawfinder-web",
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate",
    "db:studio": "drizzle-kit studio",
    "postbuild": "npx drizzle-kit migrate"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "next": "14.2.3",
    "react": "^18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "^18.3.1",
    "typescript": "^5.4.5",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.3",
    "drizzle-orm": "^0.30.10",
    "@neondatabase/serverless": "^0.9.3",
    "zod": "^3.23.8",
    "stripe": "^15.5.0",
    "@stripe/stripe-js": "^3.4.1",
    "@stripe/react-stripe-js": "^2.7.1",
    "@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.577.0",
    "@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner": "^3.577.0",
    "next-auth": "^4.24.7",
    "resend": "^3.2.0",
    "react-markdown": "^9.0.1",
    "rehype-sanitize": "^6.0.0",
    "lucide-react": "^0.378.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^20.12.12",
    "@types/react": "^18.3.2",
    "drizzle-kit": "^0.21.4",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.19",
    "postcss": "^8.4.38",
    "eslint": "^8.57.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "14.2.3"
  }
}

import { pgTable, uuid, varchar, text, integer, boolean, timestamp, jsonb } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';

export const shelters = pgTable('shelters', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 200 }).notNull(),
  address: text('address'),
  city: varchar('city', { length: 100 }),
  state: varchar('state', { length: 50 }),
  zip: varchar('zip', { length: 10 }),
  phone: varchar('phone', { length: 20 }),
  email: varchar('email', { length: 255 }),
  hours: jsonb('hours'),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const animals = pgTable('animals', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
  species: varchar('species', { length: 20 }).notNull(),
  breed: varchar('breed', { length: 100 }),
  age_months: integer('age_months'),
  size: varchar('size', { length: 20 }),
  gender: varchar('gender', { length: 10 }),
  description: text('description'),
  status: varchar('status', { length: 20 }).default('available').notNull(),
  shelter_id: uuid('shelter_id').references(() => shelters.id),
  featured: boolean('featured').default(false),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  updated_at: timestamp('updated_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const animalImages = pgTable('animal_images', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  animal_id: uuid('animal_id').references(() => animals.id).notNull(),
  s3_key: varchar('s3_key', { length: 512 }).notNull(),
  alt_text: varchar('alt_text', { length: 255 }),
  sort_order: integer('sort_order').default(0),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const adoptionApplications = pgTable('adoption_applications', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  animal_id: uuid('animal_id').references(() => animals.id).notNull(),
  applicant_name: varchar('applicant_name', { length: 200 }).notNull(),
  applicant_email: varchar('applicant_email', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  applicant_phone: varchar('applicant_phone', { length: 20 }),
  housing_type: varchar('housing_type', { length: 50 }),
  has_yard: boolean('has_yard'),
  other_pets: text('other_pets'),
  experience: text('experience'),
  why_adopt: text('why_adopt'),
  status: varchar('status', { length: 20 }).default('submitted').notNull(),
  reviewer_notes: text('reviewer_notes'),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  updated_at: timestamp('updated_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const donations = pgTable('donations', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  donor_name: varchar('donor_name', { length: 200 }),
  donor_email: varchar('donor_email', { length: 255 }),
  amount_cents: integer('amount_cents').notNull(),
  stripe_payment_intent_id: varchar('stripe_payment_intent_id', { length: 255 }),
  message: text('message'),
  is_recurring: boolean('is_recurring').default(false),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const events = pgTable('events', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  title: varchar('title', { length: 200 }).notNull(),
  description: text('description'),
  event_date: timestamp('event_date').notNull(),
  location: text('location'),
  max_attendees: integer('max_attendees'),
  rsvp_count: integer('rsvp_count').default(0),
  shelter_id: uuid('shelter_id').references(() => shelters.id),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const contactMessages = pgTable('contact_messages', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 200 }).notNull(),
  email: varchar('email', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  subject: varchar('subject', { length: 300 }).notNull(),
  message: text('message').notNull(),
  created_at: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, staging, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  lint-and-typecheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npx tsc --noEmit

  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-typecheck
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL_CI }}
          STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY_TEST }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_PK_TEST }}

# PawFinder Web

Pet adoption platform built with Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, Drizzle ORM, and PostgreSQL.

## Getting Started

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env.local
# Fill in your environment variables
npm run dev
```

## Stack

- **Framework:** Next.js 14 (App Router)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS
- **Database:** PostgreSQL (Neon) + Drizzle ORM
- **Payments:** Stripe Elements
- **Images:** S3 + CloudFront
- **Email:** Resend
- **Auth:** NextAuth.js
- **Deploy:** Vercel

## Environments

| Environment | URL | Branch |
|------------|-----|--------|
| Production | pawfinder.org | main |
| Staging | staging.pawfinder.org | staging |
| Dev | dev.pawfinder.org | dev |

## Project Structure

```
app/           → Pages and API routes (App Router)
components/    → Reusable UI components
lib/           → Shared utilities (db, s3, stripe, schemas)
drizzle/       → Database schema and migrations
public/        → Static assets
```

## Form Pattern

All forms follow a consistent pattern:
1. Zod schema in `lib/schemas/`
2. Schema shared between form component and server action
3. Client validates per-step, server re-validates on submit
4. Submissions go through server actions (not API routes)

pull_requests.title: Build adoption application multi-step form
pull_requests.head: feature/paw-10-adoption-form
pull_requests.author: matt.chen
pull_requests.body:
Closes PAW-10.

Builds the adoption application form as a multi-step wizard. Key architectural decisions:

1. **Reusable MultiStepForm component** — I built a generic `MultiStepForm` at `/components/MultiStepForm.tsx` that accepts an array of steps, each with a title, zod schema, and render function. This should be reused for any future multi-step forms.

2. **Shared zod schemas** — The validation schema lives in `/lib/schemas/adoption.ts` and is imported by both the form component (client-side validation per step) and the server action (server-side re-validation on submit). One schema, two consumers. No duplicated validation.

3. **Server actions over API routes** — Form submission goes through a server action at `/app/actions/adopt.ts`, not an API route. Server actions handle the auth session automatically, which avoids the token refresh issues we hit with API routes.

This pattern (zod schema + MultiStepForm + server action) should become the standard for all forms on PawFinder.

commits.message: scaffold MultiStepForm component with progress indicator
add adoption zod schemas for all 3 steps
implement AdoptionForm using MultiStepForm component
add server action for adoption submission with Resend email
wire adoption form into animal profile page

comments.author: sarah.kim
comments.body: Really clean pattern with the shared schemas. Quick question — do we want to add the animal's name to the confirmation email subject line?

comments.author: matt.chen
comments.body: Good idea, added. Also updated the email to include the application ID so applicants can reference it if they contact us.

comments.author: jake.rivera
comments.body: The MultiStepForm component is great. I'll use this same pattern for the event RSVP form. The progress indicator with the step circles looks really nice too.

comments.author: matt.chen
comments.body: That's the idea — one component, reuse everywhere. If you need to add features to it (like conditional steps), modify the shared component rather than building a new one.

pull_requests.title: Add Stripe donation page with Elements integration
pull_requests.head: feature/paw-11-donations
pull_requests.author: matt.chen
pull_requests.body:
Closes PAW-11.

Adds the /donate page with Stripe Elements for card input. Went with Elements instead of Checkout Sessions because:
- We need custom form styling to match our design system (the preset amounts, dedication message, recurring toggle)
- Checkout Sessions redirect to Stripe's hosted page which breaks the UX flow
- Tradeoff: more code to maintain, but full control over the form

Used the same zod schema pattern from the adoption form. Schema at `/lib/schemas/donation.ts`, shared between DonationForm and the donate server action.

Webhook handler at `/api/webhooks/stripe` records the donation in the DB after payment confirmation. Handles both one-time (payment_intent.succeeded) and recurring (invoice.payment_succeeded).

commits.message: add donation zod schema
implement DonationForm with Stripe Elements
add donate server action with PaymentIntent and Subscription creation
add Stripe webhook handler for payment confirmation
add donation page with SSR for real-time Stripe session

comments.author: sarah.kim
comments.body: Webhook handler looks solid. I tested it with Stripe CLI locally — both payment_intent.succeeded and invoice.payment_succeeded fire correctly. One thing: should we send a tax receipt email for donations over $250?

comments.author: matt.chen
comments.body: Good call — let's add that as a follow-up. For now the thank-you email is sufficient. The tax receipt has legal implications we need to check with the shelter director first.

comments.author: jake.rivera
comments.body: The donation form looks great with the preset amount buttons. The Stripe Elements card input matches our design system perfectly. Nice call going with Elements over Checkout Sessions.

pull_requests.title: Add shelter contact form
pull_requests.head: feature/paw-13-contact-form
pull_requests.author: sarah.kim
pull_requests.body:
Closes PAW-13.

Adds a contact form at /contact. Following Matt's form pattern: zod schema in /lib/schemas/contact.ts, shared between the ContactForm component and the server action.

Includes rate limiting (5 submissions/hour/IP) using an in-memory map. We can upgrade to Redis later if the in-memory approach doesn't scale.

commits.message: add contact zod schema
implement ContactForm component
add contact server action with rate limiting and Resend email
add contact_messages table migration
add contact page

comments.author: matt.chen
comments.body: Schema pattern looks correct. One note: always use server actions for form mutations, not API routes. I see you did that here — good. This should be the standard going forward for all forms. The auth token refresh issue we had in March is the reason.

comments.author: sarah.kim
comments.body: Yep, server action for the mutation, schema shared with the component. Following the pattern from PAW-10 and PAW-11.

comments.author: jake.rivera
comments.body: The rate limiting is a nice touch. In-memory is fine for now given our traffic levels.

pull_requests.title: Add pet photo gallery with S3 presigned uploads
pull_requests.head: feature/paw-12-image-gallery
pull_requests.author: jake.rivera
pull_requests.body:
Closes PAW-12.

Adds:
- Image gallery component on animal profile page (grid + lightbox)
- Presigned upload URL endpoint for staff image uploads
- Image registration endpoint
- animal_images table migration

Images are served directly from CloudFront using plain img tags. No next/image optimization per Matt's guidance.

commits.message: add animal_images migration
implement presigned URL generation in lib/s3.ts
add /api/images/presign and /api/images/register endpoints
build AnimalGallery component with lightbox
integrate gallery into animal profile page

comments.author: matt.chen
comments.body:
Gallery looks good. For the image URLs, make sure you're using the CloudFront domain from the env var, not hardcoding it. The CDN domain is different per environment.

Also, don't use next/image or Vercel Image Optimization for these. Our images are user-uploaded and often 5-8MB — the optimizer adds latency on first request and has size limits. CloudFront handles caching with a 24h TTL which is plenty since we rarely update images after upload.

comments.author: jake.rivera
comments.body: Updated to use CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN env var. Using plain img tags with the CDN URL.

comments.author: mia.torres
comments.body: The lightbox looks great on desktop. On mobile the close button is a bit small — can you make it at least 44x44px for touch targets?

comments.author: jake.rivera
comments.body: Fixed, bumped the close button to 48x48px.

pull_requests.title: Add event calendar page with RSVP form
pull_requests.head: feature/paw-14-events
pull_requests.author: jake.rivera
pull_requests.body:
WIP — Closes PAW-14.

Adds:
- /events page listing upcoming events
- /events/[id] detail page with RSVP form
- RSVP form using the standard zod schema pattern (schema at /lib/schemas/event-rsvp.ts)
- Server action for RSVP submission

Still need to:
- Add max attendee cap enforcement
- Send confirmation email via Resend
- Add calendar view option

commits.message: add events table migration
add event-rsvp zod schema
implement events list page with ISR
add event detail page with RSVPForm
add rsvp server action

comments.author: matt.chen
comments.body: Pattern looks right — zod schema shared, server action for mutation. Make sure the events list page uses ISR with revalidate=60, same as the animals browse page. Events don't change frequently enough to need SSR.

comments.author: jake.rivera
comments.body: Already set to ISR 60s. The RSVP form submission goes through a server action as you suggested in Slack.

commits.message:
scaffold MultiStepForm component with progress indicator

Generic multi-step form component that accepts steps with zod schemas. Will be reused across all multi-step forms on the site.

wire adoption form into animal profile page

Adoption form is now accessible from the animal detail page via an "Apply to Adopt" button. Form data flows through the shared zod schema to the server action.

Ref: PAW-10

add donate server action with PaymentIntent and Subscription creation

Server action creates either a one-time PaymentIntent or a recurring Subscription based on the is_recurring flag. Uses the donation zod schema for validation. Chose Stripe Elements over Checkout Sessions for UX control.

Ref: PAW-11

add contact server action with rate limiting and Resend email

Follows the same pattern as adoption and donation: zod schema shared between component and server action. Rate limited to 5 submissions per hour per IP using in-memory map.

Ref: PAW-13

implement presigned URL generation in lib/s3.ts

Presigned upload URLs expire after 5 minutes. Max file size 10MB. Accepts JPEG, PNG, and WebP. Frontend uploads directly to S3, bypassing our server.

Ref: PAW-12

implement events list page with ISR

Using ISR with 60s revalidate per Matt's guidance. Events don't change frequently enough for SSR. Consistent with the animals browse page rendering strategy.

Ref: PAW-14
